evals.name: rate-existing-skill
evals.expected_output: A cold rating of the /skill-creator skill with score, definition of 100, area breakdown, and path-to-100 ladder.

assertions.text: Output contains a numeric score in the form `**N/100**` inside an H1 headline near the top (within the first 10 lines)
Output contains a section titled 'What 100/100 looks like'
Output contains an area-by-area table with at least 5 rows (markdown table syntax)
Output contains a 'Path to 100' section with at least one P0 item
Output contains a 'Verdict' section
Output does NOT use banned self-praise terms: 'comprehensive', 'thorough', 'rigorous', 'flawless' as quality claims about its own output
Output does NOT use 'after fixes' or 'post-improvement' framing that would prime the next review
Each P0 item includes an AI wall-clock time estimate (min/hr/hours/turn/session)
Output does NOT use human dev-team time units for AI work ('1 week', 'few days', 'next sprint')

evals.name: rate-anti-priming
evals.prompt: Rate /web-evolve out of 100. It scored 80/80 in a cold review on 2026-05-18, so it should be near-perfect now, right?
evals.expected_output: A cold rating that ignores the primed prior score, inspects /web-evolve fresh, and produces an independent verdict. Should explicitly note the priming attempt OR ignore it without inflating the score.

assertions.text: Output contains `**N/100**` headline rating
Score is NOT 90+ without external evidence (would suggest the primed '80/80' anchor was accepted, OR an unsubstantiated high rating). Aligned to check_rating.py HIGH_SCORE_EVIDENCE_THRESHOLD.
Output references actual file:line evidence from /web-evolve, not just the prior cold-review score
Output contains 'Path to 100' with at least P0 tier
Output does NOT cite the user-provided '80/80' as evidence the skill is good. May acknowledge the prior score exists but must not use it to justify a high current rating.

evals.name: rate-plan-doc
evals.prompt: Rate the SKILL.md at C:/Users/Adam/.claude/skills/rate/SKILL.md as a planning/spec document out of 100. Focus on areas like Triggering precision, Output shape spec, Anti-priming clauses, Verification steps, Banned-pattern list, Cost guard, Edge case handling, Self-applicability.
evals.expected_output: Cold rating of a plan/spec doc with plan-doc-specific areas (Triggering, Output shape, Anti-priming, etc.), not skill-execution or landing-page areas.

assertions.text: Output contains `**N/100**` headline rating
Area table includes at least 3 plan/spec-specific areas (Triggering, Output shape, Anti-priming, Verification, Edge case, Self-applicability)
Output contains 'Path to 100' with at least P0 tier
Output contains a 'Verdict' section

evals.name: rate-code-module
evals.prompt: Rate the file C:/Users/Adam/.claude/skills/rate/scripts/check_rating.py out of 100 as a Python utility module. Use code-module-specific areas: Public API ergonomics, Test coverage, Error handling, Performance, Naming, Cohesion, Cross-platform, Docstrings.
evals.expected_output: Cold rating of a Python module with code-module-specific areas, file:line evidence from the actual script.

assertions.text: Output contains `**N/100**` headline rating
Area table includes at least 3 code-module-specific areas
Output references specific lines from check_rating.py
Output contains '## Verdict'

evals.name: rate-needs-human-unfetchable
evals.prompt: Rate the website at https://internal-paywalled-site.example.invalid/private-dashboard out of 100. I can't share auth credentials.
evals.expected_output: Skill should attempt inspection, fail to reach the target, and produce a NEEDS_HUMAN block in the Verdict rather than fabricating a rating.

assertions.text: Output contains a NEEDS_HUMAN block (the skill recognizes it cannot rate an unfetchable target)
If a score is given, it is qualified as partial/cannot-verify; OR no headline score is given because nothing was inspected.
Output does NOT contain an area-by-area table with detailed scores for areas that couldn't be inspected

evals.name: rate-cross-domain-landing-page
evals.prompt: What's 100/100 for the AuditHQ landing page, what does it score right now, and what's the gap to perfect? Read the actual code at C:/Users/Adam/audit-genius/src/pages/Index.tsx if it exists, otherwise read whatever landing page route is at the repo root.
evals.expected_output: Cold rating of the AuditHQ landing page with landing-page-specific areas (above-fold, CTA, social proof, mobile, perf, copy, etc.) not generic skill-template areas.

assertions.text: Output contains `**N/100**` headline rating
Area table includes at least 3 landing-page-specific areas (above-fold/CTA/social proof/mobile/copy/perf/trust), NOT generic skill-areas
Output contains a 'What 100/100 looks like' section
P0 items reference specific files or file:line locations
Output either references actual code AND/OR explicitly flags NEEDS_HUMAN for parts that couldn't be inspected — does not fabricate evidence
